title: Insight: Shein tried to turn Brazil into a production hub. Local factories walked away
description: In 2023, as Shein was taking Brazil by storm with trendy styles, low prices, and endorsements from megastars like Anitta, the company made an audacious pledge to turn the country into a manufacturing hub for all of Latin America.
extracted:
### Revenue in Brazil
- Brazil contributed $3 billion (7% of Shein's projected sales of $48 billion) in 2023, according to Coresight Research.
- Shein does not share its financial data and declined to confirm the accuracy of this estimate.
- Brazil is Shein's largest market after the U.S., and its second-largest after the U.S. in 2023.

### Market Share
- No specific market share percentages or figures stated.

### Manufacturing Efforts
- In 2023, Shein pledged to invest $150 million in collaborations to produce clothing in 2,000 local factories, aiming to generate 100,000 fashion manufacturing positions in Brazil by 2026.
- Partnered with 336 Brazilian factories by the close of 2023.
- Current number of local suppliers not disclosed.
- Only one producer still making garments for Shein: GB Manufacturing in the southeastern state of Espírito Santo (owner: Marco Britto); two other local manufacturers also supplying Shein (declined to comment, status unverified by Reuters).
- Interviews with manufacturing associations, unions, and organizations in all 12 states where factories previously operated confirmed only the above active production.
- Shein will adopt a more "selective" strategy to strengthen collaborations with "the most capable factories."
- Shein's online marketplace in Brazil supports over 45,000 local entrepreneurs/sellers.
- Six factory owners terminated partnerships after demands for significant price reductions and tighter timelines:
  | Factory Owner | Location/Details | Specifics |
  |---------------|------------------|-----------|
  | Núncio Nob de Azevedo (Nobre Confecções) | Northeastern Brazil, part of apparel consortium; employed 59 people; produced for Shein in latter half of 2023 for 6 months | Left with surplus materials sold locally; terminated due to unmanageable price/timeline demands. |
  | Medei de Araujo (Zaja factory) | Proçoara, northeastern town with 11,000+ residents; employs 128 people; produces for major Brazilian retailers and own brand; contemplated adding 50 positions | Completed initial order mid-2023; faced quicker delivery demands, reduced orders, price cuts up to 30%; skirt price from 50 BRL ($9.31) to 38 BRL ($7.07); jacket from 65 BRL to 45 BRL; not feasible in Northeast. |
  | José G. Filho (garment factory, Pernuco) | Pernambuco | Participated in Shein-sponsored tour of Chinese factories (Guangzhou, Hangzhou; 5 days); did not finalize contracts as lowest price exceeded Shein's budget. |
- Four of six former factories interviewed are in small towns where garment production is a vital economic driver.
- Shein has ~7,000 factories in China (predominantly Guangdong province).
